title: Active Inference and Robotics
subtitle: A research scaffold for embodied agents, active sensing, and robotic control.
audience: Roboticists, AI researchers, embodied cognition groups, and builders of autonomous instruments.
lede: Robotics is a natural test bed for Active Inference because perception, planning, action, and uncertainty are coupled in the same physical loop.

sections.heading: Why the domain fits
sections.body: Embodied systems must choose actions that both accomplish goals and improve the information available for future control. Active Inference makes that epistemic part of action selection explicit.

sections.heading: Application pattern
sections.body: A modular report should track the robot body, sensor model, action policy, preferred outcomes, and evaluation environment separately. That keeps a simulation result, a laboratory robot, and a field instrument from being collapsed into one claim.

sections.heading: Evidence to collect next
sections.body: The next pass should collect active sensing studies, robotic control implementations, autonomous microscopy work, and symbolic robotics examples. Each record should identify whether it is theory, simulation, hardware, or deployed instrumentation.

sections.heading: Reference Backbone
sections.body: Christopher L. Buckley, Chang Sub Kim, Simon McGregor, Anil K. Seth (2017). The free energy principle for action and perception: A mathematical review. Journal of Mathematical Psychology. DOI: 10.1016/j.jmp.2017.09.004. Thomas Parr, Giovanni Pezzulo, Karl J. Friston (2022). Active Inference: The Free Energy Principle in Mind, Brain, and Behavior. MIT Press. Giovanni Pezzulo, Francesco Rigoli, Karl J. Friston (2017). Active Inference, homeostatic regulation and adaptive behavioural control. Progress in Neurobiology. DOI: 10.1016/j.pneurobio.2017.08.001.

cards.title: Active sensing
cards.text: Use action to reduce uncertainty about objects, tasks, and operating conditions.

cards.title: Embodied policy selection
cards.text: Evaluate actions by expected outcomes and information gain rather than reward alone.

cards.title: Implementation register
cards.text: Track simulator, hardware, repository, and benchmark status separately.
